title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Fields, Framing, and Request Smuggling Defense · Inference Brief: Insecure Parser Smuggling
summary: The origin and proxy disagree about framing, forwarded fields are appended without trust, hop-by-hop fields cross boundaries, and parser tolerance is enabled to fix malformed clients. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts insecure parser smuggling increment in proxy hop, framing, and parser-consistency contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep insecure parsing disabled, align parser and normalization policies across hops, test differential cases, and close connections on ambiguous messages.
Reject conflicting Content-Length, transfer-coding ambiguity, invalid field syntax, premature EOF, excess body, and front-end or origin disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Insecure Parser Smuggling · Theory Insecure Parser Smuggling
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8096 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject untrusted topology, ambiguous framing, and insecure parser policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
